description: Fix bugs found during plugin audit: missing null guards on API response arrays that cause crashes, and a missing credentials header on the file upload fetch call.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/slack && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add missing null guards on API response arrays in list tools
userStories.description: As a plugin user, I want list tools to handle missing array fields in API responses gracefully so that they don't crash with TypeError when the Slack API omits the array field.
acceptanceCriteria: list-channels.ts: `data.channels` is guarded with `?? []` before calling `.map()` — e.g., `(data.channels ?? []).map(mapChannel)`
list-members.ts: `data.members` is guarded with `?? []` — e.g., `members: data.members ?? []`
list-users.ts: `data.members` is guarded with `?? []` before calling `.map()` — e.g., `(data.members ?? []).map(...)`
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Three list tools access API response arrays without null guards, which crashes if the Slack API returns a response without the array field. Other tools in the same plugin (e.g., read-messages.ts, read-thread.ts, search-messages.ts) already use the `(data.field ?? []).map(...)` pattern — follow that established pattern.

1. `plugins/slack/src/tools/list-channels.ts:52` — change `data.channels.map(mapChannel)` to `(data.channels ?? []).map(mapChannel)`
2. `plugins/slack/src/tools/list-members.ts:42` — change `members: data.members` to `members: data.members ?? []`
3. `plugins/slack/src/tools/list-users.ts:53` — change `data.members.map(...)` to `(data.members ?? []).map(...)`

These are one-line fixes per file. The pattern is already established by read-messages.ts:53 (`(data.messages ?? []).map(...)`).

userStories.title: Add credentials: 'include' to file upload fetch call
userStories.description: As a plugin user, I want the file upload fetch call to include session cookies so that uploads succeed in Slack environments that validate session cookies on upload URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: upload-file.ts: The `fetch(uploadResponse.upload_url, ...)` call at the upload step includes `credentials: 'include'` in its init options
The existing `redirect: 'error'` and `signal: AbortSignal.timeout(30_000)` options are preserved
cd plugins/slack && npm run build passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/slack && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
In `plugins/slack/src/tools/upload-file.ts:96-101`, the raw `fetch()` call to the upload URL does not include `credentials: 'include'`. The Slack API call at line 74 uses `slackApi()` which passes cookies via form-encoded token, but the actual file upload at line 96 uses raw `fetch()` without session cookies. Add `credentials: 'include'` to the fetch options object at line 96.

Current code:
```
const uploadResult = await fetch(uploadResponse.upload_url, {
  method: 'POST',
  body: contentBytes,
  redirect: 'error',
  signal: AbortSignal.timeout(30_000),
});
```

Add `credentials: 'include'` to this options object. All other options must be preserved.
